title: Add the --from option to bud
body:
When processing a Dockerfile supplied to the bud command,
replace any image name after the FROM statement with the
value passed in to the --new-from option.  This will not
change the value in the on-disk version of the Dockerfile,
only the one in memory for the lifetime of the build
process.

If the image name is a variable, do not replace that.

I also found our bash completions were missing several and a bad alpha ordering in the man page which has been corrected.
Addresses: #2007

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body:
I think the option should just be --from.  No reason for the --new- part.
Completions need to be updated.
Does not look like you have a test for this?
Did you try this with a complex command line?
FROM FOO as bar
FROM $ARG
I'd worked up a test but neglected to add.  Will add that and touch up the other stuff too.
@rhatdan happy green tests buttons and I think I've covered your concerns.
I thought the idea was only to change the initial from line?  Not all of the from lines?
Might be my misunderstanding, but I thought they wanted to replace all.  @tnozicka @dustymabe thoughts/input?

> Might be my misunderstanding, but I thought they wanted to replace all. @tnozicka @dustymabe thoughts/input?

I haven't dealt with multi-stage builds much so I don't know if I have a good answer. I had been only thinking about the initial from line.
Currently in OpenShift BuildConfigs/Builds it replaces the last FROM in the Dockerfile as that is likely the runtime image. I came to know only recently.
@tnozicka Do  you think that is the correct behaviour?

For simple FROM replacement, replacing the last FROM seems like a good choice to me, given it is the runtime image. 

The other option is generic replacement like `--replace-image='alias:my_image' --replace-image='registry/repository/image:tag:my_image'` (probably with a better separator then `:`)
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 09d1c24) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@TomSweeneyRedHat What is the scoop on this PR?

I've just been thinking about it.  Replacing all the FROM targets was relatively easy, replacing the last one is not something I could figure out quickly code wise.  Need to spend some time playing and haven't gotten back to it.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Needs a rebase, should we get back to this one?
I should, it just hasn't bubbled up.
